title: Italian Museum Heist: Renoir, Cézanne, and Matisse Paintings Stolen

scenes.subtitle_text: According to Reuters, thieves have stolen three paintings by Renoir, Cézanne, and Matisse from a museum near Parma, Italy
scenes.image_search_query: Magnani Rocca Foundation exterior real life photography

scenes.subtitle_text: The paintings, worth millions of euros, were taken from the Magnani Rocca Foundation after the thieves forced open the entrance on March 22nd
scenes.image_search_query: Magnani Rocca Foundation entrance real life photography

scenes.subtitle_text: The stolen works include Renoir's 'Fish,' Cézanne's 'Still Life with Cherries,' and Matisse's 'Odalisque on the Terrace'
scenes.image_search_query: pierre auguste renoir fish painting real life photography

scenes.subtitle_text: Local media reports suggest the thieves completed the heist in under three minutes, escaping through the museum gardens
scenes.image_search_query: Magnani Rocca Foundation garden real life photography

scenes.subtitle_text: The museum suspects a well-organized gang was responsible, as the alarm system was triggered during the theft
scenes.image_search_query: museum alarm system real life photography

scenes.subtitle_text: This incident follows a series of high-profile art heists across Europe, raising concerns about museum security
Art expert Claudio Strinati suggests the theft may be followed by a ransom request, a common tactic in art heists
scenes.image_search_query: art expert examining painting press photo
